observations.recommendation: We recommended management to adhere with the documentary and reporting requirements of LBC 111 dated June 10, 2016 in order for them to identify and assess whether the existing LEEs are financially self-sufficient and profitable and provide them better decision making relative to the LEEs operations.
observations.reason: Dependent on the DBM roll-out of Operation Manual for LEEs.

observations.recommendation:
Recommendation/s:
We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the:
1. Municipal Engineering Office to submit to the General Services Office the
Certificates of Project Completion of the afore-cited completed infrastructure
projects and all other projects for recording.
2. Municipal Accountant to prepare the necessary adjusting entries to close the CIP
accounts to its appropriate Property Plant and Equipment accounts and provide the
corresponding depreciation expense and accumulated depreciation.
3. Submit to the Office of the Auditor copy of the JEV taking up the reclassification
of asset accounts and the provision of the depreciation expense and the
corresponding accumulated depreciation.

Recommendation/s:
We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal Accountant to:
a. review the composition of the Accounts Payable to determine the validity of the
claims, ensure that all recorded payables are duly supported with the required
documents and drop from the books those accounts that were not supported with
necessary documents.
b. revert payables aged more than two years to be without valid claims, pursuant to
Section 98 of PD 1445.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive: 1. Direct the Municipal Development Council (MDC) to ensure thorough deliberation of additional Projects/Programs and Investment Program (PPAs) in aid of the LGU’s effort to mitigate the impacts of COVID-19, hence, this PPAs should be embodied in the local development plans to be properly endorsed to the Local Finance Committee for inclusion in the supplemental budget, together with the approved supplemental investment program. 2. If not, require a Sanggunian Resolution to rectify the expended PPAs which were not included in the Supplemental Budgets.
observations.managementAction: MDC Resolution adopting the Supplemental Investment Program No.1 wherein priority programs, projects and activities to be funded out by the Bayanihan Grant were already incorporated.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Local Chief Executive: 1. Provide justification for the absence of local ordinance for the creation of Special Account in the General Fund (SAGF) as provided by LBC No. 125 and Section 313 of RA No. 7160. 2. Direct the Municipal Accountant to secure first an authority from the Sangguniang Bayan through the enactment of an ordinance before creating Special Accounts in the General Fund.
observations.managementAction: Justification letter was submitted. The management had secured an authority from the Sangguniang Bayan to create special account in the GF for the Bayanihan Grant.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management to: 1. Adopt the minimum requirements of the laws and pertinent accounting and reporting guidelines, particularly applicable to in-kind donations received by the Municipality from various donor; and 2. Require the designated Property/Supply Officer to: a. Submit Summary/List of Donations Received, Distributed and Balances with complete supporting documents. b. Submit Registry of Donated Relief Goods for DRRM only for the remaining balances for each kind of relief goods using the prescribed format.
observations.managementAction: The management had implemented the suggested recommendation. The list was submitted but subject for updating. The Registry was submitted but subject for updating.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management: a. Non preparation and submission of project requirements - 1. Direct the concerned end-user to submit justification on non-preparation and submission of Project Requirement, otherwise these expenditures will be considered as unnecessary that will subsequently be suspended in audit. 2. Also, direct BAC to submit justification/explanation on processing the procurement of these items when these were not among the project requirement submitted by Requisitioning. b. Incomplete Supporting Documents – P8,489,610.00 We recommended that Management submit the lacking documentary requirements to verify the regularity and propriety of the procurement transactions under the Bayanihan Act. Furthermore, the management is required to submit explanation for the afore-cited deficiencies and courses of action to be taken to avoid recurrence thereof.
observations.managementAction: The management had submitted the justification letter. The management had submitted the lacking documents and explanation for the deficiencies noted.

observations.recommendation: We recommended Management to: 1. Direct the Accounting Office and General Services Office to determine the difference of their variance of records and make necessary adjustments if any. 2. Require the Inventory Committee to convene and strategize the conduct of physical count of all Properties Plant and Equipment of the Municipality to present a more accurate report. 3. Adjust the books of accounts and the property records, as warranted, after reconciliation. 4. Submit to the Office of the Auditor the Report of the Physical Count of Inventories (RPCI) not later than July 31 and January 31 of each year for the first and second semesters, respectively. b. Deviation on the policy adopted by the Municipality in the recording of supplies and materials inventories amounting to P17,198,450.75 - We recommended that Management strictly implement the perpetual inventory method in the recording of supplies and materials and other items purchased and recording all issuances therefrom as expense or other asset account using the moving average method of inventory costing, except however, in case of purchases made out of petty cash fund for immediate use which should be taken up as a direct expense. The ending balance of all supplies and materials inventory per books should then be reconciled with the physical count of such inventories.
observations.managementAction: Accounting Office and the GSO are on the process of reconciliation. The unreconciled variance had decreased. The management had submitted the RPCI for the 1st and 2nd semesters of CY 2021. The management had implemented the suggested recommendation.

observations.recommendation: We recommended management to: a. Strictly enforce the period of liquidation as prescribed above and require the immediate refund/return of any excess amount thereof; b. Demand the immediate settlement of the un-liquidated cash advances amounting to P184,732.12 from persons concerned; c. Direct the offices concerned to develop strategies to expedite collections and to enforce loan payment per provisions of the MOA/Contract. d. If the circumstances warrant, request for write-off for receivables where settlement/collectability can no longer be ascertained as prescribed in COA Circular No. 2016-005 dated December 19, 2016.
observations.managementAction: The management had implemented the suggested recommendation. According to management, collection of these accounts receivable and cash advances is very remote since the persons involved are no longer living in the area. Nevertheless, request for write-off was already done and submitted to COA but returned back due to lacking documents as required in COA Circular No. 2016-005 dated December 19, 2016.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Treasurer to: 1. Request sufficient funding for plantilla position and for other needs of the office to facilitate effective property tax collection, monitoring and assessment; 2. Refer the matter of ancestral domain to the provincial government of Compostela Valley to notify them of the problem and come up with an appropriate strategy or solution on the matter.
observations.managementAction: The management had implemented the suggested recommendation.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Local Chief Executive:
1. Direct the Municipal Development Council (MDC) to ensure thorough
deliberation of additional Projects/Programs and Activities (PPAs) in aid
of the LGU’s effort to mitigate the impacts of COVID-19, hence, this
PPAs should be embodied in the local development plans to be properly
endorsed to the Local Finance Committee for inclusion in the
supplemental budget, together with the approved supplemental
investment program.
2. If not, require a Sanggunian Resolution to rectify the expended PPAs
which were not included in the Supplemental Budgets.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive:
1. Provide justification for the absence of local ordinance for the creation of
Special Account in the General Fund (SAGF) as provided by LBC No.
125 and Section 313 of RA No. 7160.
2. Direct the Municipal Accountant to secure first an authority from the
Sangguniang Bayan through the enactment of an ordinance before
creating Special Accounts in the General Fund.

We recommended that Management to:
1. Adopt the minimum requirements of the laws and pertinent accounting
and reporting guidelines, particularly applicable to in-kind donations
received by the Municipality from various donor; and
2. Require the designated Property/Supply Officer to:
a. Submit Summary/List of Donations Received, Distributed and
Balances with complete supporting documents.
b. Submit Registry of Donated Relief Goods for DRRM only for the
remaining balances for each kind of relief goods using the prescribed
format.

We recommended that Management submit the lacking documentary
requirements to verify the regularity and propriety of the procurement transactions
under the Bayanihan Act. Furthermore, the management is required to submit
explanation for the afore-cited deficiencies and courses of action to be taken to avoid
recurrence thereof.

(Also refer to recommendations under item a above.)

We recommended management to: a. Direct the Accounting Office and General Services Office to determine the difference of their records and make necessary adjustments if any, and; b. Require the Inventory Committee to convene and strategize the conduct of physical count of all Properties Plant and Equipment of the municipality to present a more realistic report.
observations.reason:
Hampered due to COVID-19 outbreak
- The assigned personnel were deployed during DSWD-SAP beneficiaries and the record evaluation and selection.
- It takes time for the itemized/detailed adjustment of PPE accounts and its corresponding depreciations.

observations.recommendation: We recommended management to require the MEEDO to: 1. Transfer the PPEs from the General Fund-Proper books to their respective special account books (e.g. GF-MEEDO (Admin), GF-Market, GF-Cemetery, etc.) in order to present fairly not just the Financial Statement of the municipality as a whole, but also its component FS. 2. Record all depreciation expense and accumulated depreciation in the special account books and not in the General Fund-Proper. The proper recording of these accounts will reflect the true results of operation of each economic enterprise and may assist in management in its future decisions.

We recommended management to: a. Strictly enforce the period of liquidation and as prescribed above and require the immediate refund/return of any excess amount thereof; b. Demand the immediate settlement of the un-liquidated cash advances amounting to P184,732.12 from persons concerned; c. Direct the offices concerned to develop strategies to expedite collections and to enforce loan payment per provisions of the MOA/Contract. d. If the circumstances warrant, request for write-off for receivables where settlement/collectability can no longer be ascertained as prescribed in COA Circular No. 2016-005 dated December 19, 2016.
observations.reason:
Hampered due to COVID-19 outbreak.
- Temporary discontinuance of revalidation during Community Quarantine period that resulted to the implementation of necessary health protocols.

observations.recommendation: We recommended management to: a. Re-assess the projects charged against the 20% Development Fund to ensure compliance with the DILG - DBM JMC No. 2017-01, and; b. Charge expenses for repairs and maintenance against the General Fund’s Maintenance and Other Operating Expenses (MOOE).

We recommended to the management to: a. Refrain from approving Disbursement Vouchers (DVs) with incomplete documentation to preclude suspension in audit. b. Direct the BAC to ensure completeness and propriety of the required supporting documents for all contracts/ PO submitted to COA for review in accordance with COA Circular No. 2012-001. c. Advise the Accountant to adhere strictly the required basic requirements on disbursement of government funds pursuant to PD 1445 and COA Circular No. 2012-001.

We recommended management to adhere with the documentary and reporting requirements of LBC 111 dated June 10, 2016 in order for them to identify and assess whether the existing LEEs are financially sufficient and profitable and provide them better decision making relative to the LEEs operations.
observations.reason: Not Dependent on the DBM roll-out of Operation Manual for LEEs.

observations.recommendation: We recommended to the management to attach the necessary documentary requirements and strictly comply with the rules and regulations before the approval of transactions to verify its validity and propriety.

We recommended that management: 1. Coordinate with the Municipal Assessor’s Office and devise a comprehensive strategy primarily focused on the collection of delinquent property taxes to reduce the outstanding amount of delinquencies substantially; 2. Request sufficient funding for plantilla position and for other needs of the office to facilitate effective property tax collection, monitoring and assessment; 3. Refer the matter of ancestral domain to the provincial government of Compostela Valley to notify them of the problem and come up with an appropriate strategy or solution on the matter; and 4. Follow-up the progress of the Program to Segregate Titles for CLOA owners with the Department of Agrarian Reform.
observations.reason:
Installation of new software and linkage of MTO is ongoing.
Already proposed in the AIP but not yet funded.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that management direct the offices concerned to develop strategies to expedite collections and to enforce loan payment per provisions of the MOA/Contract. Moreover, if collections of these accounts are improbable, we recommended that management submit requests for authority to write-off in accordance with the provisions of COA Circular 2016-005 dated December 19, 2016.
observations.reason:
Hampered due to COVID-19 outbreak.
- Temporary discontinuance of revalidation during Community Quarantine period that resulted to the implementation of necessary health protocols.

     a. Direct  the  Accounting  Office  and  General  Services  Office  to

        adjustments if any, and;


     b. Require the Inventory  Committee  to  convene  and  strategize  the

Recommendation / s:

        special account books and  not  in  the  General  Fund-Proper.  The
        proper recording of these accounts will reflect the true results of
        operation of each economic enterprise and may assist in  management
        in its future decisions.

2016.

We recommended that management direct the Municipal Accountant to draw a journal entry to take up the cancellation of stale checks. Henceforth, strictly observe Section 59 of the NGAS Manual for LGUs on proper treatment and accounting procedures for stale checks.

We recommended the strict implementation of the perpetual inventory method in the recording of supplies and materials and other items by coursing all purchases through the inventory account for each item purchased and recording all issuances therefrom as expense or other asset account (e.g. CIP), using the moving average method of inventory costing, except however, in case of purchases made out of petty cash fund for immediate use which should be taken up as a direct expense. The ending balance of all supplies and materials inventory per books should then be reconciled with the physical count of such inventories.
We recommended that the Municipal Accountant effect the necessary adjusting entry to properly classify the abovementioned financial transactions in accordance with COA Circular No. 2015-009.

We recommended that the Local Development Council thru the MPDC ensure that the PPAs appropriated under the 20% DF are in accordance with the provisions of DILG-DBM JMC No. 2017-1 dated February 22, 2017.
Utilize the General Fund Proper account for programs and projects not chargeable against the 20% Development Fund.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant should submit the required authority from the SB for the Municipal Mayor to enter into a contract or incurred expense in behalf of the LGU to implement the projects totaling P7,856,174.47.
We also recommended that in the preparation of the Annual and Supplemental Budget, the Municipal Mayor should provide specific and sufficient details of the proposed projects/programs in compliance with COA Memorandum No. 2010-014 to avoid disallowance in audit.

We recommended management to accomplish the lacking minimum standards on disaster preparedness, and mitigation as prescribed in Disaster Preparedness Plan 2011-2028 Volume 2, most especially on the category on service delivery, and the procurement of lacking equipment be considered in the LDRRM planning to fully comply with the minimum requirements.

We recommended to the management to attach the necessary documentary requirements and strictly comply with the rules and regulations before the approval of transactions to verify its validity and propriety.
Moreover, submit the abovementioned deficiencies to prove the validity and propriety of these transactions.

We recommended that the Local School Board ensure that the proposed budget was properly balanced, reasonably distributed, and the most priority expenses which are repairs and maintenance of school buildings and other facilities of public elementary and secondary schools as stated in Section 100 of RA 7160 were greatly considered. We recommended further that the Local School Board carefully evaluate the programs/projects/ activities to be included in the SEF Budget taking into consideration the specific PPAs set forth under Sections 100 and 272 of R.A. No. 7160. Otherwise, expenditures not falling within those allowed under the said laws shall be disallowed in audit.

We recommended the following:

Submit justification letter on the above-noted deficiencies.

Allocate 1% of the IRA for the strengthening of the LCPC and implementation of PPAs and;

Establish/ and or prepare Council’s Annual Work and Financial Plan, Local Investment Plan for Children (LIPC) and prepare Municipal CPC Annual Report.

We recommended management to require the Municipal Accountant to submit the related financial reports monthly and/or quarterly for the special accounts under the General Fund, namely: 20% Development Fund, Local Disaster Risk Reduction Management Fund (LDRRMF), Economic Enterprise, and such other funds that would require separate account in the general fund in order to facilitate immediate verification on the sources and uses of these funds.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the responsible officials of the Municipality to make necessary actions to facilitate the implementation of the projects/programs/activities so that the targeted beneficiaries may enjoy the benefits to be derived therefrom.

We recommended to the Management to create adequate control measures to formulate rules and regulations in safeguarding the collections in order to prevent funds from loss or wastage. We further recommended the following courses of actions:
Use Official Receipt in acknowledging collections from Tagbibinta Falls.

Management makes sure that all Accountable Officers are properly bonded.

We recommended that management direct the offices concerned to develop strategies to expedite collections and to enforce loan payment per provisions of the MOA/Contract. 

Moreover, if collections of these accounts are improbable, we recommended that management submit requests for authority to write-off in accordance with the provisions of COA Circular 2016-005 dated December 19, 2016.
observations.reason: Request for authority to write-off was not yet approved.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the management consider the following courses of action to improve the efficiency of project implementation:
	
Direct the concerned implementing offices to address the issues/concerns that caused the non-implementation and/or delay in the completion of projects;

Create a monitoring team and closely follow through the periodic progress implementation of development projects to ensure their completion within the programmed timelines for the attainment of the planned socio-economic developmental goals and provision of desired benefits without further delay to intended beneficiaries; and

Revisit the guidelines on local planning and development and comply strictly with the conditions for the determination and actual implementation of projects that support the Annual Investment Plan to improve efficiency in the local governance as a whole.

It is recommended to direct the Municipal Agriculturist to evaluate the causes on the failure to attain the yearly targets of the program. Also, regularly monitor and supervise the implementation of the organic production and promotion project and consider the following areas of concerns:

Expand the forage area and properly maintain the existing forages to produce sufficient food for the goats not only for the existing number at hand but also for the expected number of goat heads to be produced on succeeding (3rd) year of implementation;

Goat production shall be given priority for the organic fertilizer production to achieve the targets for both of these activities. 

Vermi casts production should be enhanced, and vermi bed should be expanded to achieve target outputs.

In view thereof, we recommended Management to:
a. Require the Municipal Accountant to effect the necessary adjustment on the
omissions/errors to correct the reported balances of the affected accounts in the
Financial Statements;
b. Prepare a reconciliation statement for the discrepancies noted in the balances of
Due to other NGAs account vis-a-vis NGAs records and effect the necessary
adjusting entries, if any and coordinate or make representation to NGAs
Accountant to settle the variance.;
c. Observe the additional information required by PPSAS that is not shown on the face of the financial statements but relevant to an understanding.

We recommended that management direct the offices concerned to develop strategies to expedite collections and to enforce loan payment per provisions of the MOA/Contract. 

Moreover, if collections of these accounts are improbable, we recommended that management submit requests for authority to write-off in accordance with the provisions of COA Circular 2016-005 dated Deceember 19, 2016.
observations.reason:
Cannot locate the concerned persons in their last known address.
Some of the beneficiaries had already passed away.
The management will submit request for writing off of accounts.

observations.recommendation: Direct the Municipal Accountant to prioritize the preparation and submission of the bank reconciliation statements to facilitate timely review and verification of accounts and ensure the accuracy and existence of the recorded cash in bank balances.

We recommended that the BAC Secretariat submit the original contracts and their supporting documents to the Office of the Auditor within five (5) days from their execution, as required under COA Circular No. 2009-001 dated February 12, 2009 in order that timely and relevant technical and auditorial review and evaluation of the contract may be conducted so that the agency will be promptly informed of any deficiency found during the audit, if any.

We recommended the relief of Mr. Dario Fabillar as Municipal Economic and Development Officer (MEEDO) including his sub-collectors to prevent more losses of government funds.

Maintain a record book or logbook to record all accountable forms received and released to accountable officers.

Monitor closely all sub-collectors and see to it that collections are deposited promptly and intact. 	

Require the liquidating officer at the MTO to carefully and thoroughly check all collections, remittances and accountable forms receipts and issuances to avoid losses.

We recommended:

Direct the Municipal Accountant and Municipal Treasurer to refrain from paying transactions not fully supported with complete documents to establish validity and propriety pursuant to Section 4(6) of PD 1445.

Submit all the missing and deficient supporting documents of the disbursement vouchers audited to prove their validity and propriety and avoid suspensions and disallowances.

We recommended that the management consider the following courses of action to improve the efficiency of project implementation:
	
Direct the concerned implementing offices to address the issues/concerns that caused the non-implementation and/or delay in the completion of projects;
Create a monitoring team and closely follow through the periodic progress implementation of development projects to ensure their completion within the programmed timelines for the attainment of the planned socio-economic developmental goals and provision of desired benefits without further delay to intended beneficiaries; and

Revisit the guidelines on local planning and development and comply strictly with the conditions for the determination and actual implementation of projects that support the Annual Investment Plan in order to improve efficiency in the local governance as a whole.
observations.reason:
Some Projects remained Unimplemented and not completed (<50% completed) as of Dec. 31, 2017:
Continuing Fund (2016)–one (1)
Current Appropriation (2017) for 20% DF –fourteen (14)

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that management revisit the guidelines on local planning and development and the Joint Memorandum Circular No. 2011-1 of DILG and DBM and comply strictly with the conditions specifically identifying the projects/programs allowable to be charged under the 20% Development Fund.
Also, we required management to submit the Distribution List of the recipient of the motorcycles to ensure that actual delivery of the units had transpired.

We recommended that the Local School Board to:

Consider and include only expenditures appropriately chargeable to Special Education Fund pursuant to RA 5447, RA 7160 and DECS-DBM-DILG Joint Circular No.2 01-A and 01-B during the planning and budget preparation.

Refrain future payment of expenditures not allowed to be charged under the Special Education Fund to avoid audit suspensions/disallowances

For schools in dire need of establishing extension classes, submit request to the DepEd Division Superintendent supported with the computation of the number of pupils/students vis-à-vis number of regular DepEd teaching staff in the concerned elementary/secondary public schools in order to accommodate the salaries of teachers to be hired which can be appropriately charged against the Special Education Fund.

It is recommended to direct the Municipal Agriculturist to evaluate the causes on the failure to attain the yearly targets of the program. Also, regularly monitor and supervise the implementation of the organic production and promotion project and consider the following areas of concerns:

Expand the forage area and properly maintain the existing forages to produce sufficient food for the goats not only for the existing number at hand but also for the expected number of goat heads to be produced on succeeding (3rd) year of implementation;

Goat production shall be given priority for the organic fertilizer production to achieve the targets for both of these activities. 

Vermi casts production should be enhanced and vermi bed should be expanded in order to achieve target outputs.
observations.reason: Since the established target for goat production is too high and not attainable, the management had requested the Department of Agriculture for the revision of the set target for goat production. However, no response from the DA was received. This will be followed-up by the concerned office.

observations.recommendation:
It is recommended to direct the Municipal Accountant to prioritize the preparation and submission of the bank reconciliation statements to facilitate timely review and verification of accounts and ensure the accuracy and existence of the recorded cash in bank balances.

Enroll in the Internet Banking facility of Landbank “weAccess” which is offered to their institutional clients with services such as account inquiry, fund transfer, account sweep, payroll service, auto credit and debit. This will aid the municipality in the timely preparation of bank reconciliation statements.

Recommendations:
We recommend that management direct the offices concerned to develop strategies to expedite collections and to enforce loan payment per provisions of the MOA/Contract.
Moreover, if collections of these accounts are improbable, we recommend that management submit requests for authority to write-off in accordance with the provisions of COA Circular 2016-005 dated December 19, 2016.

Recommendation:
Direct the Municipal Accountant to prioritize the preparation and submission of the bank reconciliation statements to facilitate timely review and verification of accounts and ensure the accuracy and existence of the recorded cash in bank balances.

Recommendation:
We recommend that the BAC Secretariat submit the original contracts and their supporting documents to the Office of the Auditor within five (5) days from their execution, as required under COA Circular No. 2009-001 dated February 12, 2009 in order that timely and relevant technical and auditorial review and evaluation of the contract may be conducted so that the agency will be promptly informed of any deficiency found during the audit, if any.

Recommendations:
We recommend the relief of Mr. Dario Fabillar as Municipal Economic and Development Officer (MEEDO) including his sub-collectors to prevent more losses of government funds.
Maintain a record book or logbook to record all accountable forms received and released to accountable officers.
Monitor closely all sub-collectors and see to it that collections are deposited promptly and intact.
Require the liquidating officer at the MTO to carefully and thoroughly check all collections, remittances and accountable forms receipts and issuances to avoid losses.

Recommendations:
We recommend:
Direct the Municipal Accountant and Municipal Treasurer to refrain from paying transactions not fully supported with complete documents to establish validity and propriety pursuant to Section 4(6) of PD 1445.
Submit all the missing and deficient supporting documents of the disbursement vouchers audited to prove their validity and propriety and avoid suspensions and disallowances.

Recommendations:
We recommend that the management consider the following courses of action to improve the efficiency of project implementation:
Direct the concerned implementing offices to address the issues/concerns that caused the non-implementation and/or delay in the completion of projects;
Create a monitoring team and closely follow through the periodic progress implementation of development projects to ensure their completion within the programmed timelines for the attainment of the planned socio-economic developmental goals and provision of desired benefits without further delay to intended beneficiaries; and
Revisit the guidelines on local planning and development, and comply strictly with the conditions for the determination and actual implementation of projects that support the Annual Investment Plan in order to improve efficiency in the local governance as a whole.

Recommendations:
We recommend that management revisit the guidelines on local planning and development and the Joint Memorandum Circular No. 2011-1 of DILG and DBM  and comply strictly with the conditions specifically identifying the projects/programs allowable to be charged under the 20% Development Fund.
Also, we require management to submit the Distribution List of the recipient of the motorcycles to ensure that actual delivery of the units had transpired.

Recommendations:
We recommend that the Local School Board to:
Consider and include only expenditures appropriately chargeable to Special Education Fund pursuant to RA 5447, RA 7160 and DECS-DBM-DILG Joint Circular No.2 01-A and 01-B during the planning and budget preparation.
Refrain future payment of expenditures not allowed to be charged under the Special Education Fund to avoid audit suspensions/disallowances.
For schools in dire need of establishing extension classes, submit request to the DepEd Division Superintendent supported with the computation of the number of pupils/students vis-à-vis number of regular DepEd teaching staff in the concerned elementary/secondary public schools in order to accommodate the salaries of teachers to be hired which can be appropriately charged against the Special Education Fund.

It is recommended to direct the Municipal Agriculturist to evaluate the causes on the failure to attain the yearly targets of the program. Also, regularly monitor and supervise the implementation of the organic production and promotion project and consider the following areas of concerns:
Expand the forage area and properly maintain the existing forages  to produce sufficient food for the goats not only for the existing number at hand but also for the expected number of goat heads to be produced  on succeeding (3rd)  year of implementation;

Goat production shall be given priority for the organic fertilizer production to achieve the targets for both of these activities. 
Vermi casts production should be enhanced and vermi bed should be expanded in order to achieve target outputs.
observations.managementAction:
The management had:
Expanded the forage area from 2,000m2 to 10,000m2 
Give priority attention to breeding animals
Expand the vermi bed under shaded areas.

Actual Accomplishment for the 3rd year operation are as follows:
*Goat Production – 32 or 13.33%

*Vermi Composting – 172,300 kgs or 81.33%

*Vermi Tea – 10,504 litres or 110.19%

*Organic Concoctions – 1,211 litres or 130.66%

*Trainings – 609 pax 0r 169.17%
The management will request the DA ROXI for the revision of the targeted goat production since the established target was not attainable.
observations.reason: The established target for goat production is too high and not attainable.

observations.recommendation: It is recommended to require the Local Disaster Risk Reduction Management Council to incorporate the programs/projects/activities to be charged to the prior years’ balances of local disaster risk reduction management fund (LDRRMF) in the preparation of the annual LDRRMF investment plan pursuant to Section 4.4 of COA Circular 2012-002 dated September 12, 2012.
observations.managementAction: Unexpended balances of prior years’ LDRRMF were expended solely for projects specified in the LDRRMFIP duly approved by the MDRRMC. PPAs not included in the LDRRMFIP shall be issued with supplemental/ amendatory LDRRMFIP.

observations.recommendation: It is recommended that the management through the GAD Focal Person to seek approval of its GAD Plan and Budget and secure Certificate of Approval from the Department of Interior and Local Government (DILG) Regional office for integration of the GAD Programs, Projects and activities in its Annual Investment Plan for the year 2016 and onwards.
observations.managementAction:
For 2016 and 2017 GAD Plan and Budget, the LGU had secured Certificate of Approval from the DILG. 

The LGU is on the process of establishing Sex Disaggregated database thru Community Based Monitoring System (CBMS) implementation. They are on the stage of completion of survey and data processing/ validation and results generation.

observations.recommendation: It is recommended to require the GAD Focal Person to submit the CY 2015 Annual Accomplishment Report to the COA Auditor to be able to validate and evaluate implemented programs, projects and activities.
observations.managementAction:
The 
management already 
submitted the GAD Annual Accomplishment Report for CY 2015 on Feb. 15, 2016

observations.recommendation:
We recommend the following courses of action:
Require the Philippine Councilor Leagues (PCL) and all other leagues organized by elected officials, to issue a government Official Receipts (OR)/ Accountable Form No. 51 (AF 51) for all collections made so that proper financial accountability could be established.

PCL collection should be deposited as trust fund and should be disbursed in accordance with the board of director’s resolutions, subject to the usual accounting and auditing rules and regulations.

Ensure that all future registration fee payments for PCL conducted trainings/seminars/activities be receipted with a government official receipt which is Accountable Form 51 to avoid audit suspensions/ disallowances.
observations.managementAction: For CY 2016, the liquidation of Registration Fees for PCL conducted activities attended by the SB Members was not honored, thus, not liquidated in the books of accounts. Only the travel allowances were partially liquidated.

observations.recommendation: It is recommended that the Bids and Awards Committee (BAC) to set the cost of bidding documents in accordance with the standard rates provided in Appendix 25 of the Revised IRR of RA 9184 in order to encourage participation and competition among prospective bidders.
observations.managementAction: The management set forth the cost of bidding documents in accordance with the standard rates provided in Appendix 25 of the RIRR of RA 9184.

observations.recommendation: It is recommended to require the GSO to submit approved Purchase Orders together with their supporting documents to the Audit Team for contract review within five (5) working days from issuance as prescribed in Section 3.2.1 of COA Circular 2009-001 dated February 12, 2009 in order to have a timely review and evaluation of the various contracts entered into by the Municipality.
observations.managementAction: The POs were submitted on time by the BAC Secretariat.
